floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: volatile cast is LOAD-BEARING-likely (cse-skip-blocks folds the second lbu; the fixed CFG offers no store/call invalidation point between the reads) - verify by removal, then the se

frontier.hypothesis: UNMEASURED: the CSE fold may not actually fire (the diagnosis asserts 'would CSE' without a measurement; cse-skip-blocks has path/insn guards) - plain removal of volatile could already score 0
frontier.mechanism: cse.c skip-blocks guards (path length, LABEL_NUSES, register invalidation in the skipped block) may block the fold in this exact shape
frontier.next_probe: sandbox func_8001E800 --disable all with the volatile qualifier removed; 0 = trivial cleanup commit, non-zero = measured proof the cast is load-bearing

frontier.hypothesis: UNMEASURED: a CSE-defeat spelling that keeps both loads - second read through a re-derived address expression, a u8 local captured per-read, or restructured arms - exists within the fixed CFG
frontier.mechanism: keep the second MEM from being rtx_equal_p to the first in cse.c's hash table while global-alloc still unifies both bases into $v1 with the 0x62 constant offset
frontier.next_probe: tools/sweep_variants.py over 4-6 respellings (re-derived ptr at the flags read, per-read u8 temps, flags computed as a call-argument expression) - low prior, but cheap to falsify in one sweep

frontier.hypothesis: UNMEASURED: the +0x62 rob flags byte is genuinely IRQ/callback-touched, qualifying D_80101EC8-region access for the legitimate-volatile-interrupt-touched two-prong allowlist path (declaration-level, not cast-level)
frontier.mechanism: if a VSync/sound-tick callback writes the flags byte asynchronously, the original source plausibly declared the field volatile - type-level semantics, not coercion
frontier.next_probe: grep the interrupt/callback call graph (VSyncCallback registrants, sound tick handlers) for writers of the rob +0x62 flags byte; document findings either way

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): volatile-coercion cheat: `volatile u8 *ptr = (volatile u8 *)(&D_80101EC8 + v * 1100)` is an inline volatile cast on a non-volatile game-state global (D_80101EC8 declared extern u8 in code6cac.h:300). The cast forces GCC to emit two separate lbu loads of ptr[0x62] (target F038 + F050) that it would CSE into one without volatile. D_80101EC8 is absent from volatile_extern_allowlist.txt; no IRQ-writer citation exists; the cast form (volatile T *) is explicitly excluded from the narrow scalar-extern volatile carve-out (carve-out covers only extern volatile T G; declarations, not pointer casts). Remove the volatile cast and find a pure-C structural approach — either demonstrate genuine IRQ-touching (allowlist process) or restructure so GCC naturally emits two loads.  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)
